lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Good day and welcome back to Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: My name is Ali Morrisane - the greatest salesman in the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're the greatest salesman in the world, why are you still selling goods from a stall?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well one can only do and sell so much. If I had more staff I'd be able to sell more, rather than wasting my time on menial things. I could get on with selling sand to the Bedabin and useless tourist trinkets to just about

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: everyone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help. Adventuring is a full time job you know!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: No problem my friend, perhaps another time.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Anyway, have a look at my wares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to help you but...

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Yes, I know, I know - the life of a shop keeper isn't slaying dragons and wooing damsels, but it has its charms.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Although perhaps you can help me some other way. A nephew of mine lives not too far from here in a little town called Pollnivneach. He was supposed to become my assistant, but he hasn't shown up. If you fetch him

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: here I'll give you the first month of his salary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll find your nephew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you had better become the greatest merchant in Gielinor once I succeed!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Ah, many thanks, my friend. My nephew's name is Ali and he lives to the south in a town called Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Tell him his uncle Ali M needs his help up in Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: And while you're here, have a look at my wares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help. Adventuring is a full time job you know!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: No problem my friend, perhaps another time.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Anyway, have a look at my wares.

sections.title: Talking to Ali Morrisane before any other progress

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I sent you to learn the whereabouts of my nephew, Ali. Is there any word of him in Pollnivneach?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: That's not really the news I was hoping for.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: But since you've been gone trade has gotten even busier. Will you have a look at my wares?

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Welcome, adventurer, to the town of Pollnivneach, the gateway to Menaphos and Al Kharid. My name is Ali and I'm the mayor of this town. I hope you enjoy your stay here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. That is one of the warmest welcomes I've ever had! People generally treat travelling adventurers with suspicion.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: All are welcome here. Now more than ever I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: There's trouble in town, and a lot of villagers have left as a result. Things are looking dire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for someone called Ali.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Well, take your pick!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean I'm looking for a specific Ali. He's Ali Morrisane's nephew.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Oh, that Ali! Well, er, your best bet is to ask around town. I'm sure someone will know what he's up to.

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Camel Man

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Welcome to my discount camel store. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A discount camel store?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Yes - it's my great idea - selling camels at discounted prices so that the common man can experience the joys of owning a camel of their very own! They're not just a source of kebab meat you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I buy a camel then? I'm hungry!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: I would never sell one to the likes of you! They are beautiful, majestic creatures, not food! Get out of my shop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, camels are beautiful creatures.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Ah, wondrous camels! Best of all Saradomin's creations. Ships of the desert, far more useful than any horse or donkey. Truly, they are a man's best friend.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Perhaps you'd like a camel of your own? You're in the right place, if so...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but thanks for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want a camel? All they do is spit and...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Pah! I've seen mummies with more brains than you! If you treat camels badly then of course they're going to spit at you. May your robes never be clean again!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I was a camel in a previous life.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: You're teasing me now! You rogue!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Everyone laughs at the poor Discount Camel Seller.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is the discount camel business good then?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Business is booming! Pollnivneach has never seen this number of visitors.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: In fact I'm thinking of expanding and trying to break into the camel hire market.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: If only I could figure out how to get people to return the camels...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this town.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well, Pollnivneach is a funny little place. It's a small town and you'd think it'd be nice and quiet.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: But those Menaphites and bandits are making the place quite unbearable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In what way?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well, one group is as bad as the other! They steal, they fight, they cause trouble with us locals. Just yesterday one of those bandits came looking for protection money!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't the authorities stop this?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: We're a small town. We don't have the resources to keep the gangs off our backs! Menaphos and Al Kharid are no help either.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: To tell the truth we live in hope that an outsider, someone with no ties to either group, could broker a deal to halt the hostilities between them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the weak will always be trodden on. Why not stand up to them?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: That's a little unfair, my friend. We can't all be brave adventurers. If we were, where would you purchase quality camels at discounted prices?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: You might think about that. Anyway, isn't it the job of the brave adventurer to protect the weak and innocent from evil and harm?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could help.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Yes, perhaps you could.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know the politics better than I do. What do you recommend?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Me?! I am but a humble discount camel seller. You're the adventurer, don't you know what to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I know what to do! I was just testing you!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: That is reassuring to hear. Too often one meets brainless adventurers whose solution to every problem is to hack, maim and murder.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: I'm glad to know you're not of that ilk.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should know, but offered advice should never be shunned.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: The state of adventurers today. They need everything spelled out for them.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: What, you want me to put a little note in your quest journal?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: But fine, I'll give you a hint. Try and see if you can get the gangs to agree to a truce by offering them each a token gift as an act of reconciliation.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: If that fails to work, you must consider trying to influence the gangs from within.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: It's a beautiful day today, but word has it that it will rain tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: No! When have you ever known it to rain here in Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Anyway...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are those camels around the side for sale?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Those two camels around the side are already sold.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: I've got new stock coming very soon though. Come back later if you're still interested.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but thanks for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Ali from Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well, you've found your man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Do you have an uncle called Ali?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Why yes - all my uncles are called Ali!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear! Do any of them own a stall in the Al Kharid Bazaar?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well no, but one of them owns a moderately successful camel hire franchise.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Have you considered hiring a camel? Or perhaps purchasing one of your own? I can get you a discount.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Greetings, desert traveller. Do you require the services of Ali Morrisane's flying carpet fleet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Ali Morrisane.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: What, you haven't heard of Ali M? Possibly the greatest salesman of the Kharidian empire if not all Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold on... yes I can place him now. He has me looking for his nephew Ali, I think he needs some help with his shop.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I wouldn't be surprised, he's mentioned to me on a few occasions that he is thinking of diversifying even further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wouldn't have seen his nephew about then?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: No it's been a quite a while since I last saw him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: What do you want? Information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you know?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: No one knows what's going on in the streets better than me. I live, sleep, eat and drink here. The fact that I'm so small means that people tend to ignore me which helps even more.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, tell me about...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: They're a bad bunch, always starting fights down in the Asp and Snake. Word on the street is that they're led by some deranged priest. Nobody's ever seen them though - they do all their deals through Ali 'the

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Operator'. I reckon they're up to something, but they're such a close knit group that little ever slips out.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the bandits.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: There's not much to say, really. They're just a group of local thugs led by the biggest and strongest amongst them. If they have any other plans, I don't know about it.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Mayor.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: The mayor is a spineless coward! He never stands up to anybody. That's why the Menaphites are so strong now! He let them stay, and then they started causing trouble. When he realised his mistake, he hired a band

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: of local thugs to drive them out, but they saw how weak he was and turned on us too. Now we've got bandits and Menaphites, and the mayor isn't doing anything!

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Ali Morrisane's nephew.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: I know him! He used to kick us when he caught any of us stealing from his stall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where he is now then?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: He disappeared a little while ago. All I know is it had something to do with the gangs. Probably he scammed them. You'd have to gain their trust to find out more, m'afraid - if I get too close, they might disappear me

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: too!

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the town.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: It's boring! We're not as big as Menaphos or as powerful as Al Kharid, and we've got a really nasty crime problem. It's no wonder so many grown-ups have left.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you need any other information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Come back if you need any info about anything in the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will do thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the Ali the Barman

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Good day. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd like a drink please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on in town?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Nothing much really. About what you'd expect from a sleepy little town like ours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about those two gangs? That seems exciting.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: They're paying customers, so I won't rock the boat if you don't mind.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'm ok. Thanks for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Ali from Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Well, I'm called Ali and I'm from here. Are you looking for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have an uncle called Ali?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Yes! All my uncles are called Ali!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. Well do any of your uncles have a stall in the Bazaar in Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Um... no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well do you know if anyone here does?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: That's a difficult question to answer. I don't know anyone else's uncles.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The 'Asp and Snake'? What a strange name for a bar.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Maybe around these parts. Were you expecting it to have 'Ali' in the name?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: I did think about calling it 'Ali's', but then it'd get confused with the Camel Shop, the Kebab House, the Mayor's residence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I get your point. It's a perfectly fine name.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Ahh, a kind stranger. Get this old man a drink so that he may wet his throat and tell you of the strange happenings in this town.

sections.title: Using the first beer on him

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Now where were we? Ah yes, Ali Morrisane's wayward nephew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?! How did you know that's who I was looking for?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Er... well, he's a real scamp, that lad! I can't imagine a stranger would be asking after anyone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Well go on then, where is he?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: I'd tell you more, but my throat is drying up. This desert climate is an inhospitable one. Get me another beer so that I can continue my tale.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Get this poor, thirsty old man another drink so that he may wet his lips and continue.

sections.title: Using the second beer on him

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: You are too kind, now what were we talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ali Morrisane's nephew.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Of course! I've known him since he was a wee lad. A right little mischievous beggar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I can imagine. Do you have any more recent information about him? Where is he?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Well now, if you had just asked me that in the first place I would have told you directly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right, I'm sorry for beating around the bush. Do you know where Ali's nephew is now, though?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: I'd tell you more, but my throat is drying up. This desert climate is an inhospitable one. Get me another beer so that I can continue my tale.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Alas, my throat has dried up, and I cannot speak... perhaps another drink would help?

sections.title: Using the third beer on him

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Ahh, that hits the spot.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Now which of you two adventurers wanted to know where Ali's nephew is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're seeing double.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Is this a double? No wonder I feel sick...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, all I want to know is the whereabouts of Ali Morrisane's nephew.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Now is that his nephew from his side or his wife's? They have a huge family you see. I remember...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, no. Stop right there. You are not going to fool me into buying you another beer without first giving me some useful information.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: But my lips are so dry, how can I possibly continue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if your lips are dry, I'll have to wring you like a wet cloth and squeeze out enough booze to fill another tankard. I'd reckon it would hurt though.

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: I see. Don't worry about me, I'll just have to soldier on. Now what do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For the last time, where is Ali Morrisane's nephew?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Oh, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Wait, wait! The last I heard he was in trouble with those gangs hanging around town. You won't get any answers out of them, though, not while they're feuding. Maybe if you were to solve that little problem...

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Anyway, is there any chance of another beer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think?

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: What were we talking about again? Yes yes, when I was a boy... no, that's not it.

sections.title: Using an item other than a beer on him

lines.speaker: Drunken Ali
lines.text: Eh? What's this? I don't want that, get me a beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to get to the bottom of this feud between you and the Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just trying to understand why thieves of your repute would stoop to their level.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Well, if memory serves me correctly, I think the hostilities began when they robbed one of our camels. A heinous crime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if they offered a camel back as a gesture of goodwill, would you and the rest of the bandits cease your hostilities?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Those Menaphites would never do that. They have no sense of honour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if they did?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to figure out the feud that exists between you and the Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing much, I'm trying to understand why thieves of your repute would stoop to the level of the Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: Well if memory serves me correctly. I think my granddad mentioned that they robbed one of our camels. A heinous crime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if they offered a camel as a gesture of goodwill would you and the rest of the bandits cease your hostilities?

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: Those Menaphites would never do that. They have no sense of honour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if they did?

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to figure out the feud that exists between you and the Bandits.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Not much to figure out there, the problem is those thieving bandits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In what way?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: They are always stealing, starting fights and disrupting the lives of the townsfolk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Word has it that the Menaphites are also involved in unsavoury behaviour.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Well, we also steal, start fights, and disrupt lives - but we do it with class! And the bandits definitely started it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did 'they' start it?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: I can scarcely remember, it's been so long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on try.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: I think our leader once mentioned that they robbed one of our camels. A heinous crime around these parts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if they offered a camel as a gesture of goodwill would you agree to cease hostilities?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Those bandits would never agree to that. They couldn't afford even the mangiest excuse for a camel, never mind the desert traversing, four legged beauty that we would demand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if they did?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Well, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe if I got both gangs a camel, I could use the mutual good will to find out what happened to Ali's nephew.

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Camel Man

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Welcome to my discount camel store. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are those camels around the side for sale?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Those two camels around the side are already sold.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: But you are in luck - I have another two beautiful camels arriving soon from the best stable in all of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent! Although another two ugly camels would have been fine, I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me a little about them?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well Sandy, the female camel, is a placid beast. At 20 years of age she comes from a prestigious line of camels - her mother was part of the Emir's baggage train!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Lumps, on the other hand, is a 15 year old male. He's a bit surly and prone to bouts of stubbornness. However, he more than makes up for this failing with his immense strength and stamina.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Both of them are of the 'Camelus Horribleus' family, a rare breed only found in this area of Gielinor. It is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok, that's enough! They're camels, I get it.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but thanks for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What price do you want for both of them?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: One cannot put a price on these beauties, they are precious beyond the worth of the crass value of gold coins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I have them for free then?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Are you trying to be funny?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could make another offer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would 200 gold coins for the pair of them do?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Pah! That might buy you a mangy, flea-infested sheep if you're lucky. Don't insult me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could make another offer?

lines.speaker: Player

lines.text: Yeah, those pesky bandits!

lines.text: Yes, of course, those pesky bandits should be taught a lesson.

lines.text: Good choice my friend, together we will crush them.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Slow down, slow down. First you must prove yourself capable and trustworthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I manage that?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Don't worry about that, I'll just set you a few jobs to do.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: As you might have gathered we have a less-than- sparkling reputation around here.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: So I want you to foster this reputation and grow it.

lines.text: What exactly does that mean?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Tell me when you are ready. This deed should not be undertaken lightly.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.text: Give me my money!

lines.speaker: Street urchin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, of course not.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Good! Now, the next thing you have to do is a spot of burglary.

lines.text: The only place worth pilfering around here is the mayor's villa.

lines.text: I want you to retrieve his wife's jewels.

lines.text: Well I think that a disguise would be a good start. After that I would stake out the place to make sure he's not in.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator

lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.text: That man is a fool. Now give me the jewels.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I seem to have misplaced them.

lines.text: You are trying my patience. Hurry back with the jewels. Don't disappoint me.

lines.text: Now, your final task is one of the upmost importance. It has come to my attention that there is a traitor in our midst.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Yes, and I want you to root them out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why me?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Because I feel like I can trust you. You don't have the emotional baggage that comes with playing the villain your entire life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can count on me to find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aw! You can count on me to find the traitor.

lines.text: Well then get to it.

lines.text: How is the search for the traitor going?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: I just can't believe one of my friends could be a traitor.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Well, I suppose there is Traitorous Ali.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Traitorous Ali'?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Oh yes. The most untrustworthy, backstabbing little rat to scamper across Tumeken's fair desert. He'd sell his own mother to slavers if he hadn't already betrayed her and had her locked up for the last job he pulled.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose the name is a bit of a giveaway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a little too simple, are you sure it was him?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Positive, he was turned by the offer of a set of waterproofs and a ride on a camel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see he drives a hard bargain.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: Well what can I say? We don't call him 'Ali of the Good Decisions', do we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have discovered the identity of the traitor. It was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Traitorous Ali!

lines.text: No! I would never have suspected Traitorous Ali.

lines.text: But I'm sure it took much more for him to betray us!

lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Sheesh! You'd make a terrible hag. Just bring me a snake with teeth in its head - and don't even think of giving me that snake charmer's pet worm, it's about as dangerous as a rubber knife.

lines.text: I have the snake. What else do you need?

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Ah yes, that snake will do nicely. Hello, you wriggly little cutie!

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Who's a vindictive little beast? You are! You are!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, yes. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Now that I have the toxin I need something to hide its trace. Fresh camel dung should do the job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? That's disgusting.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Do you want the poison or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, fine, I'll get some camel dung.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: And make that fresh camel dung! Nice and stinky. Now hand over that venomous little darling before it takes a bite out of you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops! I almost forgot!

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Hag again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Hag.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Good day to you too, Blockhead.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Do you have the camel dung yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still working on that.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Well don't come back until you have it!

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Hag with Ughthanki dung

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Hag.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Good day to you too, Blockhead.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Here's your poison!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that was quick!

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Actually I already had it brewing. I just needed the ingredients for another batch.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: You wouldn't believe the demand. It's been murder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not even remotely funny.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Sorry, I've been dying to say that for ages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just stop. Thanks for the poison and good day to you.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Wait! Just remember, this poison isn't designed to be used on a weapon. In fact it's not a contact poison at all. You'll have to do more than just smear it on a blade!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got it. Thanks again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Hag.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Good day to you too, Blockhead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if I could have another batch of poison?

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Why? Was the first batch not good enough?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well no, I was just being greedy and wanted another batch for no good reason.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Well if that's the case, no! I have better things to be doing than continuously brewing poisons for people who have no use for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the first batch.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: What? How do you just lose a batch of the most potent poison in all of Gielinor? It's even more deadly than the jungle karambwan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long and not very interesting story.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Well alright, I'll give you another batch this once.

sections.title: Using the Hag's poison on the table

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Hag.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Good day to you too, Blockhead.

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Have you good reason to disturb me again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope!

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: I warned you about needlessly bothering me. That's it! I'm turning you into a frog!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ribbit, ribbit!

lines.speaker: Ali the Hag
lines.text: Don't taunt me, fool! Once I find a spare minute you'll be ribbiting on the other side of your face, you mark my words!

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Is the deed done?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Almost, his drink is poisoned. As soon as he gets back, he'll be done for.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Excellent work! Now, go speak with our boss.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: He wants to discuss the future direction of the gang.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: What did the boss say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't spoken with him yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ali the Operator said you wanted to see me.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: That's right. I have kept a close eye on your progress. I'm impressed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are your plans for the bandits?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: The bandits? A mere nuisance, insignificant to my overall goal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, if you're not warring with the bandits, what are you planning?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: World domination! First Pollnivneach, then Al Kharid, then the whole of the desert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: You do understand who we are, don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I thought you were a ragtag band of good-for- nothing thieves sucking the life out of this innocent town.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Silence! We are the descendants of assassins, the heirs of the revolutionaries who defeated the corrupt pharaoh and his brood!

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: For centuries we have wandered the desert, rebuilding the empire we were promised!

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Will you join us in our quest to defeat the thralls of Tumeken and reclaim our birthright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have this irrational hatred for megalomaniac, despotic zealots! Prepare to meet your maker, you big jerk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, your great-great-great-great-grandparents were the ones who killed Osmumten?

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't even know his name, and you claim to be the heirs to his empire? Come on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're just a bunch of petty tyrants looking for an excuse to hurt people.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Be careful what you say! We are loyal servants of the Devourer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope so, because you're about to meet her in person. This is for Mehhar!

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Fool! You'll never get to me. Guards protect me.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: You treacherous jackal! Prepare to die!

sections.title: Talking to the Menaphite Leader again

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: Guards!

lines.speaker: Tough Guy
lines.text: Grr!

lines.speaker: Menaphite Leader
lines.text: You haven't seen the last of me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No doubt I haven't seen the last of you... but what about Ali's nephew?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the villagers will feel more talkative now that I've rid them of these evil Menaphites. Who knows, they might even give me a reward!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: You treacherous jackal! I should never have trusted you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I say, you live and learn.

lines.speaker: Menaphite Thug
lines.text: I will avenge my master!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there!

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Hi! Do you want to go rob some more people? I could do with some more money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need to try a different tactic. Sorry, kid!

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Well do you need any information then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I think I have a fair idea of what is going on in the town now.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: I don't know what we did to deserve your presence here, but I for one am filled with regret.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's some show of gratitude! Can't I even get a 'thank you' for ridding your town of one of the gangs?

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Do you understand the concept of consequences?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I think so?

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Well, the consequences of your little piece of butchery are that the town is now even worse off. The bandits no longer have to waste time and energy fighting with the Menaphites and can direct all their attention

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: towards making us miserable. So for your efforts I thank you, but I wish no-one had asked for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll set this town right before I leave, I promise.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Why do I wish that you hadn't said that? Your heavyhanded tactics scare me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Welcome! Your actions surprise me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: It was brilliant the way you got rid of the Menaphite leader.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: So will you run the rest of them out of town now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: Welcome. Our leader would be pleased to congratulate you on your disposal of the Menaphites who plagued this town.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Why did you have to go and ruin everything? We had a beautiful future ahead of us, the town would have been ours!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I wanted to make it MY town!

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: I see. I knew the hunger for power would get to you eventually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Evil megalomaniacs set on destruction should be opposed.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: Really? I thought you were meant to follow those types blindly, with unquestioning loyalty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, Gielinor is full of power mad evil-doers. I'm sure another will come along soon enough.

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: No... it just wouldn't be the same. I think I'll just wallow in self pity for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to upset you. Can we still be friends?

lines.speaker: Ali the Operator
lines.text: No, I think I'll just wallow in self pity for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: You! You're the one that just made everything worse for us poor villagers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?! I just single-handedly took apart one of the gangs that was ruining your town!

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: You adventurers! You never think, you just act. You think that by killing a powerful bad person, Gielinor will become a better place.

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: It's not that simple, things never are. You've created a vacuum, and now all that power will go to whoever's strong enough to take it.

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Who do you think is stronger? Our elderly mayor, or the leader of the bandits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops. Sorry, I was just trying to help. So are things really that much worse?

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Yes. Before your intervention the two gangs pretty much cancelled each other out. They spent most of their energy trying to kill each other.

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Now that the balance is lost, the Bandits reign unopposed! They no longer have to deal with the Menaphites and heap misery upon misery on us. You must finish the job that you have started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, this adventuring lark is a lot more complicated than I thought!

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: If you're prepared to change something you must also be prepared to deal with the consequences! Now hurry up and fix this mess you created!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by fix the mess I created?

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Do I have to spell it out to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you please?

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Just run the bandits out of town. Incidentally, I spotted their leader to the north-west just a few minutes ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

sections.title: Running the Bandits out of town

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: Welcome! Our leader wants to congratulate you on your swift disposal of the Menaphites who plagued this town.

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Come here my friend, so I may embrace you. You have done us a great turn by disposing of those Menaphites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't jump to conclusions, I didn't do it for you.

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Regardless, I am still very grateful. Now I have the town at my mercy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I'm here to talk to you about.

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Do you want a reward? A fee? I will not begrudge you that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm here to give you an ultimatum: either leave the town of your own free will or I will make you.

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: How the worm has turned. This no-name adventurer who was robbing, looting and poisoning not too long ago is now taking the moral high ground! Or are you trying to keep the whole town for yourself?

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Pollnivneach is mine. You won't run me out without a fight. If you want us gone, you must defeat our strongest warrior in battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Start packing your bags!

lines.speaker: Bandit champion
lines.text: Grrr

sections.title: Talking to the Bandit Leader again

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Ali, come here!

lines.speaker: Bandit champion
lines.text: Grrr

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have defeated your champion, now get out of town.

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Adventurer... I have underestimated you. Perhaps we can come to an agreement. How about I work for you? Think of the riches you would make.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't try my patience. Tell me where Ali is, and then get out of here?

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Ali? Which Ali?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ali Morrisane's nephew! I know you gangs had something to do with his disappearance, so tell me where he is!

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Ali Morrisane?! You fool. You've been had!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Go have a chat with these precious townspeople of yours. You'll soon learn the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go talk to a villager now, just to prove you wrong. Now get out of here before I really get angry with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I told you to clear off! Why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Adventurer... I have underestimated you. Perhaps we can come to an agreement. How about I work for you? Think of the riches you would make.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't try my patience. Get out of here, and never return to this town again!

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Ha! You fancy yourself the people's champion, do you? You are so naive. You don't even realise you've been tricked!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Bandit Leader
lines.text: Go have a chat with these precious townspeople of yours. You'll soon see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go talk to a villager now, just to prove you wrong. Now get out of here before I really get angry with you.

lines.speaker: Bandit
lines.text: You dog! You killed our champion and for that you will die!

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: You dog! You killed our champion and for that I hope you die a slow and painful death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that? Are you looking for a fight?

lines.speaker: Cowardly Bandit
lines.text: Me? No, no, I was just, er, complimenting your, that is - don't kill me, please!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't waste my time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now are you satisfied?

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Oh, it's you again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have delivered you from two evil tyrants. Where's my thank you?

lines.speaker: Villager
lines.text: Thank you? For what? You come into our town. You rob, mug, burgle and kill and then expect respect and gratitude? Look, if it's praise and thanks you're looking for, talk to the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder why I bother sometimes! This place has been nothing but trouble and I still haven't found Ali's nephew.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: My thanks, adventurer. Your methods and motives may be questionable, but none can argue that you have delivered our little town from the wicked gangs who plagued it. I think things will change around here, now

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: that our problems are at a manageable size.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: The least that I can do for you is answer your questions about the whereabouts of Ali Morrisane's nephew. He always was a mischievous young man, full of harebrained schemes.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: He dreamed of being the greatest salesman in all of Gielinor! Unfortunately, he was a little too good.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: He angered a number of people by coercing them into buying things they didn't really want. Completely within the law, of course, but the law could only protect young Ali for so long.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Once he angered the Menaphites and the bandits, I knew I could no longer protect him.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Under cover of darkness I smuggled him out of the town, and he's been gone ever since.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: I can assure you, however, that he is safe and well - and now that you have effectively ended the threat posed by the gangs, he can continue his business elsewhere. Last I heard he planned to open some kind

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: of trade network with forty of his friends. A 'treasure trove', he claims. I'll believe it when I see it.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: I hope that helps you in your quest. If you speak to Ali Morrisane, do tell him that I wholeheartedly apologise for the way he - er, his family - has been treated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I may be able to help with one other thing. I heard that your house was burgled recently. If you're interested in recovering the goods, you ought to have a word with Ali the Operator.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Now, how would a passing adventurer know where to find my missing jewels?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, let's just put it down to adventurer's intuition.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Thank you once again. Have you spoken to Ali Morrisane yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No not yet.

lines.speaker: Ali the Mayor
lines.text: Perhaps you should! I'm sure he's keen to hear word of his nephew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Good day. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on in town?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Well since you gave the two gangs a good kicking, business has really taken off! Both gangs are spending all their gold here drowning their sorrows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great! So do I get a cut of the profits?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Get out of here, you cheeky rascal. A cut of my profits? As if!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The 'Asp and Snake'? What a strange name for a bar.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Maybe around these parts. Were you expecting it to have 'Ali' in the name?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: I did think about calling it 'Ali's', but then it'd get confused with the Camel Shop, the Kebab House, the Mayor's residence...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I get your point. It's a perfectly fine name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell Traitorous Ali that someone has poisoned his drink?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: What?! Poisoned drinks, in my bar?! My reputation would be destroyed if someone died here. Thank you adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem at all.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Here, have a beer - it's on the house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks but no thanks, I don't drink.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Don't say that around here too loud please, it may catch and then I'd be out of business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could always open up a non alcoholic fruit juice bar. You'd have less problems with drunken customers

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: You might be onto something there, but then what would I do with the drunken sailor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Drunken Ali. Oh never mind it's a long story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yuck! I don't like beer.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Fine, suit yourself. There's no need to insult me.

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Barman again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Ali the Barman
lines.text: Good day. Can I help you with anything?

sections.title: Talking to Ali the Camel Man

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Hello! Welcome back to my discount camel store. How are those camels getting along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Ali!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Hello adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any news?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Not much really, I just want to thank you for what you have done for this town. I might even stick around now that business is better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any grand plans?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well I've been debating whether I should diversify into camel rental, too. Although I do have all these cages lying around... do you think there's a market for discount jackals?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe! Best of luck, in any case.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hello again my friend. I have heard nothing from my troublesome nephew. Have you any news of his whereabouts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I do. I was able to eventually learn of his fortunes, although it was a little bit more challenging than I originally thought.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well then please, tell me what has become of him. Nothing bad I hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was able to discover that he is quite the salesman. Perhaps too good. He has roughly forty people working under him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately a number of people didn't approve of his sales techniques and as a result he is in hiding.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: You make an old man very happy. I'm so proud of young Baba.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he's considered a thief!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Yes, by people with too much money and not enough sense. All willingly parted with their money and received what they purchased.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well, it seems I still am in need of an assistant...

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: ..but take the reward anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've just been thinking Ali, there seems to be a few parallels between yourself and your nephew. Both of you are unscrupulous salesmen, both of you come from the same town and both of you no longer trade there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were you sent into exile too?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I have my reasons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, now hang on, this seems to me like one of the strangest quests I have ever been involved with. I do not believe that a businessman like you would not know of a competitor, particularly a competitor as talented as

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: your nephew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what was your motive to get me to visit Pollnivneach? Was it concern for the towns people? Hmm. I don't think so, I'd say you would probably sell your grandmother if you could get a good price for

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: her. What was the reason?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: My my, you surprise me, you're not as dumb as you look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely you didn't do all this to sell me a fake beard and headpiece.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Ah, once again you underestimate me. I sold you far more than that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Well I did buy some other odds and ends off you.... no hang on! I get it now, you're in league with Ali the Camel seller, the Kebab seller and the mayor aren't you.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I like the word 'tourism'. Did you not enjoy your stay in Pollnivneach? Was there not action, intrigue and fun to be had, and did you not notice all the other tourists there too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must apologise for ever doubting you. You really are the greatest salesman in all of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Now that we both agree on that, may I show you my wares?
